output.reason: First QA run - no previous snapshot to compare

build.details: Build completed successfully with only asset size warnings (not specific to Text block)

lint.details: No lint errors in Text block files. Theme-wide warnings exist in other blocks but none in Text.tsx or Text.php

attributes.details: All attributes defined in block.json with correct types and defaults. Attributes: anchor (string), eyebrow (string), heading (string), description (string), buttons (array)

testedScenarios.result: All fields render correctly with ThemeHeading visible and all inner blocks displayed
ThemeHeading renders with only heading populated, other fields hidden gracefully
ThemeHeading column correctly hidden when hasContent = false. Only discourse column renders.
ThemeHeading with md:sticky md:top-8 positioning. Long inner blocks content displays correctly.
Partial ThemeHeading content renders correctly without description or buttons
Anchor attribute correctly applied as id='test-anchor' on section element
Multiple content types (headings h3, paragraphs, lists, quotes, separators) all render correctly within discourse wrapper

interactiveBehavior.reason: No interactive elements - block is static content layout only

accessibility.patternCompliance: skipped - no WAI-ARIA patterns required (static content block)

checks.result: pass - ThemeHeading uses h2 (headingSize=2), InnerBlocks template starts with h3. Proper hierarchy maintained.
pass - theme_block_props() automatically adds aria-labelledby pointing to heading ID when heading exists. No aria-labelledby when hasContent=false (acceptable for content blocks).
pass - Inner blocks support proper ul/ol elements via core/list
pass - ThemeHeading buttons render as <a> elements (links for navigation)

keyboardNavigation.details: All links are keyboard accessible. No custom interactive elements. Natural tab order follows visual order.

colorContrast.details: Block uses theme CSS variables and discourse wrapper for text styling. No custom colors that could create contrast issues.

images.details: No images in this block. Inner blocks support images via core blocks with proper alt text handling.

markupDuplication.details: No duplicated markup for responsive behavior. Single DOM structure uses CSS (md:grid-cols-2) for responsive layout.

details.phpExtractPattern: pass - No manual $attributes extraction, uses auto-extracted variables
details.phpDocblock: pass - Has @var annotations for all camelCase variables: anchor, eyebrow, heading, description, buttons, children, block
details.htmlStructureSync: pass - TSX and PHP produce identical structure

additionalChecks.result: pass - Both use <section> with identical classes: 'text-block py-10 sm:py-16 not-discourse'
pass - TSX uses (displayContent || hasContent) for editor UX, PHP uses $hasContent. Logic matches: eyebrow || heading || description || buttons with url/postId
pass - Both use identical grid: 'grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-x-16 gap-y-9 items-start'
pass - Identical props passed to ThemeHeading in both TSX and PHP
pass - Both wrap inner content in <div className/class='discourse'>
pass - Bug fix applied: both TSX and PHP now include 'not-discourse' class on section wrapper

details.allowedBlocks: core/paragraph, core/heading, core/list, core/quote, core/separator, takt/button-row
details.contentRenders: pass - all inner block types render correctly within discourse wrapper on frontend

details.demoContainers: pass - all instances wrapped in takt/demo-container
details.staleMarkup: pass - no stale HTML (block is dynamic, renders via PHP)

variationsCovered: Full Content - all ThemeHeading fields + rich inner blocks
Long Content - Sticky Test - md:sticky md:top-8 behavior
Eyebrow and Heading Only - partial ThemeHeading
With Anchor ID - anchor attribute deep linking
Rich Inner Blocks - multiple content types (headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, separators)

passedChecks: Build compiles without errors
No lint errors in Text block files
Block registration valid (schema, naming, file references, attributes)
All attributes defined with correct types
Empty state handling works correctly (hasContent conditional)
Long content with sticky positioning works
Partial content (eyebrow + heading only) renders correctly
Anchor attribute correctly applied for deep linking
Multiple inner block types (headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, separators) all render
Semantic HTML structure (section, h2, h3 hierarchy)
ARIA labeling automatic via theme_block_props
No markup duplication
TSX/PHP sync validated - identical structure and classes
not-discourse class fix applied to both TSX and PHP
hasContent logic matches between TSX and PHP
PHP docblock includes all camelCase variables
Inner blocks configuration correct (allowed blocks, template, no lock)
Test page has 7 variations covering all scenarios
All variations wrapped in demo-container
No stale markup or unresolved placeholders

bugFixesVerified.verification: PASS - Both TSX and PHP now have 'not-discourse' class on section wrapper. Prevents discourse styling from bleeding into section wrapper.

bugFixesVerified.description: TSX missing hasContent conditional for ThemeHeading
bugFixesVerified.verification: PASS - TSX now has (displayContent || hasContent) conditional wrapping ThemeHeading. When block is deselected and empty, ThemeHeading is hidden matching PHP frontend behavior. When selected, ThemeHeading shows for authoring UX.
PASS - PHP template now has comprehensive docblock with @var annotations for all variables: anchor, eyebrow, heading, description, buttons, children, block. Prevents PHP_CodeSniffer warnings.

performanceNotes: Block is static content layout - no JavaScript required on frontend
Uses InnerBlocks for content authoring - leverages WordPress core blocks
No N+1 queries - no dynamic data fetching
Sticky positioning uses CSS (md:sticky md:top-8) - no JS scroll listeners
